title: Once viewed as a rental, could Viktor Arvidsson be ideal ‘bridge’ forward for Bruins?
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Viktor Arvidsson, second in 5-on-5 goals for Bruins, remains with the team after trade deadline.
Coach Marco Sturm values Arvidsson's contribution, citing his dedication and performance in the team's playoff push.
Bruins consider Arvidsson for a potential 3-year extension amid retooling efforts, with $17 million in cap space available.

title: 5 interior offensive linemen options for Patriots after Garrett Bradbury trade
author: Conor Roche
bullets: Patriots trade center Garrett Bradbury to Bears, creating a need for a new left guard this offseason.
Isaac Seumalo is viewed as the top free agent guard but may be costly for the Patriots at $8 million.
Draft prospect Emmanuel Pregnon could be a potential Day 2 pick, highlighting strong college performance and athletic ability.

title: He’s running the Boston Marathon for the ‘amazing’ Make-A-Wish children
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Jason Perry, 48, from Stockton, California, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for the Make-A-Wish Foundation.
Perry aims to raise money and awareness for Make-A-Wish children in Massachusetts and Rhode Island.
His motivation stems from meeting inspiring Make-A-Wish children and their families over 15 years.

title: Insider predicts Patriots sign Seahawks standout pass rusher, but don’t land Alec Pierce in free agency
author: Conor Roche
bullets: Patriots reportedly aim to sign Seahawks edge rusher Boye Mafe to a three-year, $48 million deal with $25 million guaranteed.
Insider predicts Colts wide receiver Alec Pierce will sign with the Chargers, not the Patriots, for $80 million over three years.
New England has $61.6 million in cap space ahead of NFL free agency, positioning them to pursue key players.

title: Review & setlist: With sharp sarcasm, Jesse Welles took on war, bullies, and Trump at the House of Blues
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Jesse Welles performed 20 songs at House of Blues in Boston on March 6, 2026.
Welles' performance included politically charged songs and covers of Black Sabbath and Radiohead.
His setlist featured his popular TikTok songs and a country rock rendition of Black Sabbath's 'Paranoid.'

title: Masataka Yoshida hits a home run in Japan’s win vs. Korea, with Drake Maye in attendance
author: Conor Roche
bullets: Masataka Yoshida hit 2-for-4 with a home run and three RBIs in Japan's 8-6 win over Korea during the World Baseball Classic.
Yoshida's third-inning solo homer followed by hits from Shohei Ohtani and Seiya Suzuki boosted Japan's lead to 5-3.
Patriots quarterback Drake Maye attended the game in Tokyo, spending time in Japan while his brother plays basketball in Kobe.

title: This Roslindale native is running her first Boston Marathon for Mass. Eye and Ear
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Makayla Aboujaoude-Quirk, a 25-year-old from Boston, is running her first Boston Marathon in 2026.
She is participating with Team Eye and Ear to support Mass Eye and Ear, where she's been a patient for nearly 10 years.
Aboujaoude-Quirk grew up in Roslindale and teaches in Boston Public Schools, fulfilling a lifelong dream to run the marathon.

title: It was overdue, but Tim Legler was promoted to ESPN/ABC’s top NBA broadcast team this season, and he’s thriving
author: Chad Finn
bullets: Tim Legler, ESPN's longest-tenured NBA analyst, joined the top NBA broadcast team this season with Mike Breen and Richard Jefferson.
Promoted in August 2025, Legler will be part of the team calling the NBA Finals in June on ESPN/ABC.
Legler praised the Celtics' performance and is eager to see Jayson Tatum's return impact alongside Jaylen Brown.

title: Cooper Flagg was the Maine story in Jayson Tatum’s return
author: Chad Finn
bullets: Cooper Flagg debuted at TD Garden with the Mavericks, scoring 16 points, 8 rebounds, and 6 assists.
Jayson Tatum returned from a 298-day absence, contributing 15 points, 12 rebounds, and 7 assists for the Celtics.
Maine native Flagg received a warm welcome from Celtics fans despite their team's 120-100 victory over the Mavericks.

title: Charles Barkley impressed by Jayson Tatum in return, but doesn’t view Celtics as favorites in East
author: Conor Roche
bullets: Jayson Tatum returned after 298 days from an Achilles tear, scoring 15 points and contributing 12 rebounds in Celtics' win.
Charles Barkley remains skeptical of the Celtics as Eastern Conference favorites, maintaining his pick of the Cavaliers.
Cleveland Cavaliers have an 8-1 record since acquiring James Harden, strengthening Barkley's support for them in the East.
